reasoning: The flagged 'unsafe' verdict stems from a false positive. The build-ui-linux.sh script containing sudo commands isn't executed during AUR build/install (not referenced in PKGBUILD functions or install hooks). All other files show standard safe patterns.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged build-ui-linux.sh as unsafe due to sudo commands, failing to recognize it's a development script unused in AUR packaging
coverage_issues: Both models skipped binary tarball analysis (netbird-0.63.0.tar.gz)
Deepseek missed systemd service files reviewed by Qwen
false_positives: build-ui-linux.sh flagged by Qwen: Not executed during makepkg, standard upstream build script
learnings: Improve context awareness: Models should prioritize PKGBUILD-invoked scripts over unused source scripts
Enhance execution context analysis: Explicitly prompt models to verify if scripts are called by PKGBUILD/install hooks
Binary analysis: Develop method to scan tarballs for embedded scripts/hooks
Reduce over-reaction: Train models to distinguish between development scripts vs packaging-executed code
